question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() does not validate that the Topic's timestampRange is non-null, causing a NullPointerException or incorrect behavior in downstream history-reconstruction logic that relies on the timestampRange to determine the topic's historical state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() does not validate that the Topic's id is non-null, causing a NullPointerException or incorrect behavior in downstream history-reconstruction logic that relies on the id to identify the topic in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() does not validate that the Topic's createdTimestamp is non-null (it is nullable per @Column(updatable = false)), causing downstream history-reconstruction logic to treat the topic as having no creation event in Hashgraph history, effectively erasing its origin?
